findings.title: update_nvr uses PUT nvrs (plural) but the integration API may require PUT nvrs/{id} — untested against live hardware
findings.suggested_fix: Run a live test against the UCK-G2-Plus in readwrite mode: PUT /proxy/protect/integration/v1/nvrs with a benign payload (e.g. {"name": current_name}). If the API returns 404 or 405, change the path to nvrs/{id} where the id comes from a preceding get_nvr() call. Add an integration test for update_nvr in test_protect_live.py once the correct path is confirmed.

findings.title: get_nvr return shape assumption: integration API GET nvrs may return a list, not a single object — callers and type annotation assume dict
findings.suggested_fix: The live integration test test_get_nvr_returns_identifier passes (PR body says 3 pass), which empirically confirms the response is a dict. However there is no unit test asserting the shape; add an explicit assertion in the integration test: assert isinstance(nvr, dict), not just any(key in nvr for key in ...). If the API ever returns a list, validate_connection will succeed (get_nvr returns without error) but every downstream caller treating the return value as a dict will silently misbehave. Low-cost guard: add assert isinstance(result, dict) inside get_nvr and raise UniFiError on list shape.

residual_risks: update_nvr is a write operation behind the 'write' tag (disabled in readonly mode) so the bad path cannot be triggered in normal readonly deployments. Risk is bounded to explicitly readwrite deployments exercising protect_update_nvr.
test_list_events_returns_list is marked xfail(strict=True) but has no @pytest.mark.asyncio — asyncio_mode=auto covers this, confirmed by the full unit suite passing. Not a bug.

_url() trailing-slash handling: prefix ends with '/' and per-method paths have no leading slash (e.g. 'nvrs', 'cameras'). f'{prefix}{path}' produces correct double-slash-free URLs in all current call sites. A future caller passing a leading slash would produce a double slash, but no such caller exists in the current codebase.

The URL-contract test (TestProtectURLContract) calls list_cameras() and asserts url__startswith the expected prefix. This is not circular because EXPECTED_PREFIX is a hardcoded literal independent of the module constant, so a future silent prefix rewrite would be caught.

bootstrap and events are known 404 on integration v1 and are documented in CLAUDE.md and the module docstring. The tools remain registered and will surface ToolError to callers — not a silent failure, and tracked in #130.
testing_gaps: No integration test for update_nvr — the only verified write path against live hardware. At minimum a readonly-mode guard test (which exists) but no live PUT test confirms the correct endpoint shape.

No unit test asserts that get_nvr returns a dict rather than a list. The type annotation and docstring both assert single-object response, but no assertion guards against a shape change in the integration API response.

test_list_events_returns_list is xfail(strict=True) but is only meaningful against live hardware. The unit test mocks return a list successfully, so the xfail is only exercised in integration mode. This is correct and expected, but worth noting that the strict xfail will become a test suite failure (XPASS) if #130 ships a fix and this marker is not removed.
